title: RFA Sir Galahad
summary:
Two ships of the Royal Fleet Auxiliary have been named Sir Galahad, after the knight of Arthurian legend.

RFA Sir Galahad (1966) (L3005), a landing ship (logistic), was lost in the Falklands War
RFA Sir Galahad (1986) (L3005), a landing ship (logistic) named for (and given the same pennant number as) the previous Sir Galahad, was involved in the 2003 invasion of Iraq
text:
Two ships of the Royal Fleet Auxiliary have been named Sir Galahad, after the knight of Arthurian legend.

RFA Sir Galahad (1966) (L3005), a landing ship (logistic), was lost in the Falklands War
RFA Sir Galahad (1986) (L3005), a landing ship (logistic) named for (and given the same pennant number as) the previous Sir Galahad, was involved in the 2003 invasion of Iraq
